description:
Document the Followr.ai platform interface for building or updating a Cowork automation skill.

Objective: Explore the Followr.ai web app at https://app.followr.ai and produce a structured documentation file covering all platform features, UI elements, and integration statuses.

Steps:
1. Open https://app.followr.ai/home in a browser tab. Ensure you are logged in.
2. Click the brand/company dropdown in the top navigation bar. List every brand name visible in the dropdown.
3. For each brand in the dropdown:
   a. Switch to that brand by clicking its name.
   b. Navigate to https://app.followr.ai/integrations
   c. Record all integrations in three categories: Needs Attention (expired), Active Integrations (with expiration dates), and Available to Connect (not yet linked).
4. Switch back to the first brand. Navigate to https://app.followr.ai/calendar and click 'Create Post' (top right). Document every field in the post creation modal: platform selection dropdown options, text area, character limits, AI generation buttons, media upload, scheduling controls, draft/publish/schedule modes.
5. Go to https://app.followr.ai/calendar. Document the calendar layout (week view, navigation, platform filter pills, any scheduled posts).
6. Go to https://app.followr.ai/posts. Document available filters (Created At, Publish At, Status, Users), post statuses (Published, Error, Draft, Pending), total post count, and per-post actions.
7. Go to https://app.followr.ai/company-settings/profile, then visit each settings sub-page: /brand-style, /brand-voice, /integrations, /webhooks, /prompts, /byok, /access, /advanced. Document what each section configures.
8. Go to https://app.followr.ai/hub/messages. Document inbox features: search, filters, platform filter, conversation list, reply capabilities, assignment.
9. Go to https://app.followr.ai/analytics. Document time range filters, platform filters, and what metrics are shown (or note if data is empty).
10. Go to https://app.followr.ai/media. Document media library tabs and asset counts.

Output: Save a structured markdown document to the outputs folder titled 'followr-ai-platform-docs.md'. Include all URLs visited, all brand names, all integration statuses per brand, all UI field descriptions, and any known issues discovered (e.g., expired integrations, error states).

Constraints:
- Do not modify any settings, create posts, or change integrations — this is a read-only documentation task.
- If a page requires a plan upgrade to access, note that and move on.
- Include timestamps and the account email (dangolden@gmail.com) in the document header.
